Descriptions.ShortDescription: Real-time dithering analysis with quality assessment and dither settings optimizer
Descriptions.LongDescription:
Comprehensive dithering analysis plugin for N.I.N.A. with real-time statistics, visual charts, and experimental quality assessment.

# Core Features
* Real-time dither settle time tracking and visualization
* X/Y pixel drift scatter plot with cumulative movements
* Statistical analysis (avg, median, std dev, min/max, success rate)
* Live updates during imaging sessions

# Experimental Quality Assessment
⚠️ EXPERIMENTAL: Advanced mathematical analysis of dither patterns using:
* Centered L₂ Discrepancy - Global uniformity measurement from quasi-Monte Carlo theory
* Voronoi Cell CV - Local spatial distribution and clustering analysis
* Drizzle Gap-Fill Metrics - Sub-pixel coverage predictions for 1×, 2×, 3× drizzle
* Combined Quality Score - Overall pattern quality rating with recommendations

Minimum 4 dithers required for quality metrics. Assessments improve with 15+ positions.

# Experimental Dither Settings Optimizer
⚠️ EXPERIMENTAL: Analyzes post-dither guiding data to recommend optimal settle parameters:
* Three profiles: Quality (1.5σ), Balanced (2.0σ), Performance (3.0σ)
* Settle Pixel Tolerance: Calculated from running RMS and standard deviation
* Minimum Settle Time: Derived from time-to-first-stable guiding per dither event
* Auto-updates after each dither event, requires minimum 3 events

# Multi-Session Statistics Persistence (NEW in 1.4)
Optionally carry all statistics over across NINA sessions:
* Toggle in the Statistics panel (default: off), state survives restarts
* All charts, statistics, quality metrics and optimizer data are restored exactly as they were when NINA was closed
* Subsequent dithers accumulate on top of the restored state
* Clear Data resets everything including the optimizer analysis

# Requirements
* N.I.N.A. 3.0 or higher
* .NET 8.0 Runtime
* PHD2

# Getting Help
Visit the #plugin-discussions channel on [NINA Discord](https://discord.com/invite/rWRbVbw) or file issues at the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/Thierrytsch/NINA-DitherStatistics/issues).
